questions.question:
Processes P1, P2, P3, P4 arrive in that order at times 0, 1, 2, and 8 milliseconds respectively, and have execution times of 10, 13, 2, and 9 milliseconds respectively. Shortest Remaining Time First (SRTF) algorithm is used as the CPU scheduling policy. Ignore context switching times.

Which ONE of the following correctly gives the average turnaround time of the four processes in milliseconds?

A. 22
B. 15
C. 37
D. 19
questions.to_find: Average turnaround time of all four processes under SRTF scheduling.

understand.plain: Four processes arrive at different times and each needs a certain amount of CPU time. SRTF always runs whichever ready process has the least remaining burst time, preempting the current process if a new arrival is shorter. Turnaround time for each process is the time from its arrival until it finishes.

keywords.explain: A preemptive CPU scheduling algorithm that always selects the process with the smallest remaining burst time. When a new process arrives, if its burst is less than the remaining time of the running process, it preempts immediately.
keywords.example: If P1 has 8 ms remaining and P3 arrives with only 2 ms burst, P3 preempts P1 at once.

keywords.explain: Total time from when a process arrives in the ready queue until it completes execution. Turnaround = Completion time - Arrival time.
keywords.example: If P1 arrives at t=0 and finishes at t=12, its turnaround time is 12 ms.

keywords.explain: Forcibly removing the CPU from the currently running process and giving it to another process with a shorter remaining burst.
keywords.example: P1 is running; P3 arrives with a shorter burst; P3 preempts P1.

understand.visual_alt: Gantt chart showing SRTF execution: P1 runs t=0 to 2, P3 preempts and runs t=2 to 4 (finishes), P1 resumes t=4 to 12 (finishes), P4 runs t=12 to 21 (finishes), P2 runs t=21 to 34 (finishes).

given.aim: Find the average turnaround time of processes P1, P2, P3, P4 under SRTF scheduling.

terms.term: Arrival times: P1=0, P2=1, P3=2, P4=8 ms
terms.meaning: The time at which each process enters the ready queue.
terms.connects: Determines when each process can first be considered for scheduling.

terms.term: Burst times: P1=10, P2=13, P3=2, P4=9 ms
terms.meaning: The total CPU time each process needs to complete.
terms.example: P3 needs only 2 ms of CPU time.
terms.connects: SRTF picks the process with the smallest remaining burst; P3's tiny burst causes it to preempt P1 at t=2.

terms.meaning: At every event (new arrival or process completion), recheck all ready processes and run the one with the shortest remaining time.
terms.example: At t=2, P3 arrives with rem=2 which is less than P1's rem=8, so P3 preempts.
terms.connects: Drives the exact execution order and completion times.

terms.meaning: Turnaround Time = Completion Time - Arrival Time
terms.example: P1 completes at 12, arrived at 0, so TT = 12.
terms.connects: Used to compute per-process TT and then the average.

given.plan: Simulate SRTF step by step at each event point (arrival or completion). At each event, choose the ready process with the least remaining burst. Record completion times, compute each process's turnaround time, then average the four values.

steps.title: Simulate SRTF: t=0 to t=2
steps.formula_raw: Run process with min remaining burst among ready processes
steps.apply:
t=0: Only P1 is ready (burst=10). P1 starts running.
t=1: P2 arrives (burst=13). Ready: P1(rem=9), P2(rem=13). P1 has shorter remaining time. P1 continues.
t=2: P3 arrives (burst=2). Ready: P1(rem=8), P2(rem=13), P3(rem=2). P3 has the shortest (2 < 8 < 13). P3 preempts P1.
steps.note: P3's very small burst (2 ms) causes an immediate preemption of P1 as soon as P3 arrives at t=2.

steps.title: Simulate SRTF: t=2 to t=4
steps.apply:
t=2: P3 starts running (rem=2).
t=4: P3 completes. Completion time of P3 = 4. Turnaround P3 = 4 - 2 = 2 ms.
Ready queue: P1(rem=8), P2(rem=13). P4 has not arrived yet (arrives at t=8). Shortest is P1. P1 resumes.
steps.note: P3 finishes quickly and P1 resumes since P4 has not arrived yet.

steps.title: Simulate SRTF: t=4 to t=12
steps.apply:
t=4: P1 resumes (rem=8). P2(rem=13) is waiting.
t=8: P4 arrives (burst=9). Ready: P1(rem=4), P2(rem=13), P4(rem=9). P1 has shortest remaining (4 < 9 < 13). P1 continues.
t=12: P1 completes. Completion time of P1 = 12. Turnaround P1 = 12 - 0 = 12 ms.
Ready queue: P2(rem=13), P4(rem=9). Shortest is P4. P4 starts.
steps.note: At t=8 when P4 arrives, P1 still has only 4 ms remaining which is less than P4's 9 ms, so P1 is not preempted.

steps.title: Simulate SRTF: t=12 to t=34
steps.apply:
t=12: P4 starts (rem=9). P2(rem=13) waiting.
t=21: P4 completes. Completion time of P4 = 21. Turnaround P4 = 21 - 8 = 13 ms.
Ready queue: P2(rem=13). P2 starts.
t=34: P2 completes. Completion time of P2 = 34. Turnaround P2 = 34 - 1 = 33 ms.
steps.note: No preemption occurs after t=12 as no new processes arrive; P4 then P2 run to completion.

steps.title: Compute Average Turnaround Time
steps.note: P3's very short burst (2 ms) dramatically reduces its turnaround time, pulling the average down.

solution.result: Average turnaround time = 15 milliseconds. Correct option: B. 15

questions.formula_note: SRTF (preemptive SJF): at each scheduling event select the ready process with minimum remaining CPU burst. Turnaround Time = Completion Time - Arrival Time; Average TT = sum of all turnaround times divided by number of processes.
